goal: Prove a generic, incremental canonical React visual compiler that emits exact native Vue, Svelte, and Solid for Button, Checkbox, Field, and Popover, with sub-second warm focused proof and no component IDs in target lowerers.

spikes.question: Can the TypeScript compiler API extract nested JSX structure, symbols, class expressions, defaults, state branches, imported icons, and compound parts deterministically?
Can an oxc-based frontend extract equivalent facts materially faster with acceptable symbol-resolution annotations?
Can trusted canonical journeys capture deterministic DOM, accessibility, classes, geometry, computed presentation, focus, events, and pixels, mapped to stable part IDs?
Which DOM-first, part-first, or dialect-based IR preserves all facts with the simplest generic target lowering and best cache granularity?
Can one winning IR generate Vue, Svelte, and Solid without component IDs in target lowerers?
Can content-addressed component shards, persistent dependency workspaces, persistent browser pages, and impact analysis reach alien-like warm latency?

hardRules: Experiments may not weaken, replace, or fabricate current conformance receipts.
No React runtime dependency in downstream output.
No component IDs or component-specific branches in target lowerers.
No copied final HTML, screenshot-to-code inference, gallery repair CSS, innerHTML/@html, or post-mount reconstruction.
One writer per exact path; maximum four parallel workers.
Every result is deterministic, content-addressed, benchmarked, and includes exact commands and failures.
A failed approach is recorded and retired rather than patched indefinitely.

success.staticFrontend: Winning frontend extracts all required structure for four components with deterministic repeated bytes and source provenance.
success.runtimeTracer: Every required state and viewport yields deterministic canonical trace and screenshot digests with stable part mapping.
success.ir: One validated IR represents all four components/states without target-framework concepts or information loss.
success.lowering: Generic Vue/Svelte/Solid lowerers contain no component IDs and produce native package shards.
success.behavior: All required trusted interaction checkpoints match canonical observations with zero diagnostics.
success.pixels: All four components, all required states, all three native targets, and all three viewports have zero unmasked differing pixels.
success.coldPerformance: Cold focused compile and proof is measured and below 10 seconds on the recorded machine.
success.warmPerformance: Warm focused compile plus exact browser proof is below 1 second, with a stretch target below 500ms.
success.fullSpike: All required artifacts exist, all gates pass, final results select winners quantitatively, promoted integration plan names exact existing compiler paths, and all changes are committed.

stopCondition: Stop only when results/final.json is passed, all required artifacts validate, every exact behavior/pixel cell is green, performance budgets pass, target lowerers contain no component IDs, full spike tests pass, and intended changes are committed.
